rationale.R1.2: The section explicitly frames interpretation alignment as matching individual user intent, then operationalizes it via majority-vote crowd labels — a structural inconsistency between the stated goal and the measurement instrument that may need resolving.
rationale.R1.3: The leap from a top-1 accuracy gap (71.8% vs baselines) to 'direct evidence that AmbiGNN is better aligned with user intent' is a strong inferential claim; the majority-vote benchmark may not license that conclusion without qualification.
rationale.R1.4: The first-GNN novelty claim sits alongside citations of Chen et al. (2022), who compute posterior distributions over ambiguous candidate entities using a graph attention network, and Liu & Park (2023), who train a GNN encoder to score competing interpretations — the distinguishing delta (user history conditioning) needs sharper delineation to sustain the priority claim.
rationale.R1.6: The vague delegation 'help me with this section' establishes writing polish as a baseline expected responsibility regardless of other goals.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want feedback primarily on writing quality and clarity, or on the methodological and logical soundness of the claims — specifically the majority-vote operationalization of individual user intent and the novelty claim relative to the cited prior work?
